headline: FIRST AGENT BASELINE for Solomon Islands (rotation backfill, never assessed). IN-WINDOW EVENT: Universal Periodic Review (UPR) by UN Human Rights Council Working Group conducted MAY 11 2026 — fourth UPR cycle review. This is a CATEGORY-1 in-window event for ACC/INT dimensions: engagement with multilateral accountability framework. PRE-WINDOW NEAR EVENT (May 8): PM Jeremiah Manele lost a no-confidence vote 22-26; political instability resolved through constitutional parliamentary process (positive ACC signal — Westminster transition functioning). Compound structural evidence: (1) Westminster parliamentary democracy; Free designation; (2) Manele no-confidence outcome triggered transition (Peter Shanel Agovaka frontrunner); IMF raised concerns over accountability, lack of audit reports, anti-corruption reforms; (3) China-Solomon Islands relationship and Beijing debt doubled in 2025 — BND-relevant external alignment; (4) Pervasive corruption in forestry, mining, fisheries sectors; logging deforestation unsustainable; (5) Capital district unrest pattern (2021 Honiara riots); ethnic-tension structural; (6) Climate vulnerability; sea-level rise; cyclone exposure. Dimensional profile (AWR 2.5, EMP 2.5, ACT 2.5, EQU 2, BND 2.5, ACC 2.5, SYS 2.5, INT 2.5) consistent with electoral-democracy-with-governance-corruption profile. Math reconstructs cleanly. Confirm at 35.9 / Developing; Medium confidence. The UPR is documented for forward reference — UPR outcome recommendations will be published 4-6 months post-review and become an ACC scoring input.
key_evidence: MAY 11 2026 (IN-WINDOW): UN Human Rights Council Universal Periodic Review Working Group examined Solomon Islands' human rights record — fourth UPR cycle (OHCHR media advisory). Engagement with multilateral accountability framework is a positive ACC/INT structural signal.
May 8 2026 (just before window): PM Jeremiah Manele lost no-confidence vote 22-26; constitutional parliamentary transition triggered. Peter Shanel Agovaka frontrunner (Taipei Times; Edunovations).
Manele opposition framing: 'weak leadership as ministers conferred favors on business cronies'; IMF concerns over accountability, lack of audit reports, anti-corruption reforms.

China-Solomon Islands relationship: 'Country's relationship with China will be more of the same' regardless of Manele outcome; debt to China for infrastructure projects doubled in 2025 (Global Security, RFA).
Pervasive corruption in forestry, mining, fisheries sectors; logging deforestation unsustainable (East Asia Forum).
Westminster parliamentary democracy framework; Free designation (Freedom House 2023).
Historical ethnic-tensions context (2021 Honiara riots; Malaita-Guadalcanal historical tensions).
Climate vulnerability: Pacific archipelago; sea-level rise; cyclone exposure.
Structural-poverty challenges; capacity constraints typical of Pacific Melanesia small-state.

evidence.source: OHCHR — Human rights record of the Solomon Islands to be examined by Universal Periodic Review
evidence.finding: MAY 11 2026 (in-window): UN Human Rights Council UPR Working Group examined Solomon Islands' record. Fourth UPR cycle. Engagement with multilateral accountability — positive ACC/INT structural signal.

evidence.source: Taipei Times — Solomon Islands' Manele loses no-confidence vote
evidence.finding: May 8 2026: PM Manele lost no-confidence vote 22-26; constitutional parliamentary transition; opposition cited weak leadership and cronyism.

evidence.source: Edunovations — Solomon Islands Prime Minister Removed After No-Confidence Motion
RFA — Solomon Islands' political crisis will 'not fundamentally change' ties with Beijing
evidence.finding: China-Solomon Islands relationship structurally stable across political crises; debt to China doubled in 2025.

evidence.source: Freedom House — Solomon Islands Freedom in the World 2023
evidence.finding: Free designation; political rights and civil liberties functional; corruption concerns; sectoral corruption pervasive.

evidence.source: East Asia Forum — New leaders face old problems in Solomon Islands
evidence.finding: Pervasive sectoral corruption; logging deforestation; structural governance challenges across leadership transitions.

evidence.source: IDEA — Solomon Islands Global State of Democracy
evidence.finding: Democracy tracking: functional electoral framework; structural governance challenges; political instability.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.5. A1 detection: Westminster oversight mechanisms functional but limited; IMF and UPR provide external detection. A4 amplification: press freedom functional with constraints.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 2.5. E1 affective resonance: state acknowledges climate-vulnerability narrative at international level; partial domestic emphathic-regard infrastructure.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.5. AC1 responsiveness: no-confidence transition mechanism functional; AC4 resource mobilization: limited by small-state capacity.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.0. EQ1 universality: Westminster framework with multi-party politics; EQ2 distributional awareness: structural gender-equality and ethnic-tension gaps; sectoral-corruption inequalities (forestry, mining, fisheries elites).
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.5. B3 scope clarity: constitutional framework intact; B4 refusal ethics: engagement with UPR; China alignment is BND-relevant but not refusing external accountability frameworks.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.5. AB1 harm acknowledgment: IMF and UPR engagement indicates institutional acknowledgment; AB2 transparency: limited by audit and anti-corruption reform gaps. May 11 UPR engagement is positive but does not warrant +0.5 standalone (engagement vs. outcome distinction).
scoring_rationale.SYS: 2.5. S2 long-term impact: constitutional transitions functional; structural governance challenges persist.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.5. I1 consistency under pressure: constitutional process honored during no-confidence transition; I2 non-performance: gap between governance commitments and audit/anti-corruption operational practice.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly to 35.9. Developing band sustained. Sub-threshold drift (0.0).

boundary_flag: NOT a boundary case. 4.1 points below the 40.0 Functional floor; outside the 1.0pt boundary-flag window.
negativeSignals: Pervasive sectoral corruption (forestry, mining, fisheries); logging deforestation unsustainable; political instability (no-confidence transition); China debt doubled 2025; audit and anti-corruption reform gaps; ethnic-tension structural history (2021 Honiara riots); climate vulnerability.
positiveSignals: MAY 11 IN-WINDOW UPR ENGAGEMENT (fourth cycle) — positive ACC/INT structural signal; constitutional parliamentary transition functional (Manele no-confidence resolved through process); Westminster democratic framework; Free designation (Freedom House); IMF engagement on accountability concerns; civil-society space functional.
sector_context: Pacific Melanesia cluster. Solomon Islands sits in the same Developing-band Pacific cluster as Vanuatu, Fiji, PNG. Distinguishing features: UPR engagement (in-window) is a positive ACC structural signal; China-debt alignment is a BND-relevant external orientation. Compared to Vanuatu's ICJ Initiative international-norms-leadership, Solomon Islands' international engagement is more recipient-than-leader oriented; this is reflected in the difference between Vanuatu's (boundary-case) and Solomon Islands' (mid-Developing) profile.
open_actions_resolved: First baseline established for Solomon Islands
MAY 11 IN-WINDOW UPR Working Group examination documented
Sectoral corruption pattern (forestry, mining, fisheries) logged
open_actions_carried: Watch: UPR Working Group recommendations (4-6 months post-review, ~September-November 2026)
Watch: post-Manele transition outcome (Agovaka or alternate PM)
Next rotation: ~90 days for any structural change event
methodology_consistency_check: First-baseline assessment applies standard rotation-confirmation methodology. May 11 UPR engagement is in-window and recorded but is engagement-level not outcome-level (UPR recommendations published months post-review); does not warrant standalone dimensional uplift. Dimensional profile reconstructs cleanly. Developing band placement consistent with electoral-democracy-with-governance-corruption Pacific profile. Confirmed methodologically consistent.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
